question_text: Calculate the current flowing through a circuit when a 12V power supply is connected to a resistor with a resistance of 4Ω. Use the formula: Current (I) = Voltage (V) ÷ Resistance (R). Give your answer in amperes (A).
explanation: Using Ohm's law, I = V ÷ R, we substitute the values: I = 12V ÷ 4Ω = 3A. This is a straightforward application of the formula that Year 8 students learn to apply in circuit problems.
